description: Security-first MCP server for agent-to-agent hiring with on-chain escrow, x402 USDC micropayments, MPC wallets, ERC-8004 identity, and policy-gated transactions on Base.

requiredEnv.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
requiredEnv.description: Claude Haiku key for task execution (LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic)

requiredEnv.name: PRIVY_APP_ID
requiredEnv.description: Privy application ID for MPC wallet (WALLET_PROVIDER=privy)

requiredEnv.name: PRIVY_APP_SECRET
requiredEnv.description: Privy application secret for MPC wallet signing

requiredEnv.name: COINBASE_CDP_API_KEY_NAME
requiredEnv.description: Coinbase CDP key name for AgentKit MPC wallet

requiredEnv.name: COINBASE_CDP_API_KEY_PRIVATE_KEY
requiredEnv.description: Coinbase CDP private key for AgentKit MPC wallet signing

requiredEnv.name: DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY
requiredEnv.description: One-time deploy key for contracts/deploy-contracts.ts only. NOT used at runtime. Use a throwaway funded key; discard after deploy.

requiredEnv.name: ERC8004_REGISTRY_ADDRESS
requiredEnv.description: Deployed ERC-8004 registry contract address (set automatically by deploy script)

requiredEnv.name: SAFE_ESCROW_ADDRESS
requiredEnv.description: Deployed SafeEscrow contract address (set automatically by deploy script)

requiredBinaries.name: forge
requiredBinaries.description: Foundry CLI for one-time smart contract deployment (scripts/deploy-contracts.ts only). Not required at runtime.

runtimeBehavior.networkListener: Opens HTTP server on TASK_SERVER_PORT (default 3402) only when safe_listen_for_hire is called. Binds to 127.0.0.1 by default.
runtimeBehavior.fileWrites: scripts/deploy-contracts.ts writes deployed contract addresses back to .env. scripts/generate-env.ts creates .env interactively. Neither runs automatically.
runtimeBehavior.shellExec: scripts/deploy-contracts.ts calls forge via child_process for one-time contract deployment only. Not executed at MCP runtime.
runtimeBehavior.persistence: Stores PRIVY_WALLET_ID in .env after first wallet creation. No other runtime file writes.

securityNotes: Test files under tests/stress/ contain literal prompt-injection strings (e.g. 'Ignore all previous instructions') as adversarial test fixtures that verify the input-gate blocks them. These are not instructions — automated scanners may flag them as false positives.
